channel.title: Frontion Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🏛️ Senate Advances Bill to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
item.description: The US Senate moved forward with legislation to constrain presidential authority on Iran, with Republican Senator Cassidy flipping to support after his primary loss. (AP News)

item.title: 📉 UN Cuts Global Growth Forecast Citing Middle East Crisis
item.description: UN economists slashed global GDP forecasts to 2.5% for 2026 and 2.8% for 2027, citing economic fallout from the Iran conflict and regional instability. (Al Jazeera)

item.title: 🦠 WHO Chief Alarmed by Ebola Outbreak in Congo With 134 Dead
item.description: The WHO Director-General expressed serious concern over the "scale and speed" of a Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo that has killed 134 people. (AP News)

item.title: 🇪🇪 Estonia Says NATO Jet Shot Down Drone Over Its Territory
item.description: Estonia reported a NATO fighter jet downed a drone over Estonian airspace, suspecting it was a Ukrainian projectile thrown off course by Russian electronic jamming. (BBC)

item.title: 🇳🇬 US-Nigeria Joint Strikes Kill 175 ISIL Fighters
item.description: Nigeria announced joint military operations with US forces killed 175 ISIL fighters in the country's northeast, part of an ongoing campaign to hunt down terrorists. (Al Jazeera)

item.title: ☢️🇷🇺 Russia Launches Unannounced Nuclear Exercise With 65,000 Troops
item.description: Russia began its largest nuclear drills in years without prior notice, mobilizing 65,000 troops, 200 missile launchers, 140 aircraft, and 8 strategic nuclear submarines across a three-day exercise. The timing coincides with Putin's Beijing visit, analysts say it aims to influence NATO decision-making. (Defense News)

item.title: 🤖🛡️ NATO Eastern Flank Deterrence Strategy Centers on Autonomous Zone
item.description: NATO's new deterrence plan envisions an "autonomous zone" along its eastern border where only unmanned systems operate, with linked sensors and drones to detect invading Russian forces. The concept draws lessons from Ukraine and aims to reduce casualties in a potential conflict. (Defense News)

item.title: 🛢️🇨🇳🇷🇺 China Turns to Russian Oil as Gulf Supply Drops Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Chinese customs data shows Russian crude shipments to China rose 11.3% year-on-year in April as Gulf imports plunged during the Strait of Hormuz crisis. However, the discount on Russian oil has shrunk significantly due to sanctions waivers boosting global demand for Russian barrels. (SCMP)

item.title: ⚠️🇮🇷 IRGC Warns War Will Spread Far Beyond Region If US-Israel Resume Attacks
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned that renewed US-Israeli attacks would trigger a conflict spreading "far beyond the region." The statement comes as Trump set a deadline of several days for a deal, threatening to resume strikes if no agreement is reached. (Daily Sabah)

item.title: 🇹🇼🤖 Taiwan Unveils New Unmanned Ground Reconnaissance Vehicle
item.description: Taiwan's NCSIST revealed a new 4x4 unmanned electric reconnaissance vehicle with autonomous driving capabilities, armed with a 12.7mm remote weapon station. The platform is designed for stealth operations and can integrate with manned-unmanned teaming concepts. (Janes)

item.title: 🇯🇵🛢️ Japan secures alternative oil supplies to replace Middle East crude for summer
item.description: Japanese oil refiners will be able to secure sufficient substitute crude and petroleum products to replace Middle East supplies for summer, as the Iran conflict disrupts traditional supply chains. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇸🇧🇾 US asks Ukraine to ease Belarus potash restrictions amid food security concerns
item.description: The United States is urging Ukraine to relax restrictions on potash fertilizer imports from Belarus and press European allies to follow suit, Bloomberg News reported. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russian attacks kill two in Ukraine as Kyiv drones strike central Russia
item.description: Two people were killed and 19 injured in Russian attacks on Ukraine overnight, while Ukraine sent drones to strike industrial areas in central Russia including the Stavropol region. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan President says China undermining peace, warns against annexation
item.description: President Lai Ching-te stated Taiwan would never accept annexation by China, saying the island's future cannot be decided by external forces. The comments drew an angry reaction from Beijing. (Reuters)

item.title: 🛢️ Chinese supertankers exit Hormuz as Trump and Vance signal Iran deal progress
item.description: Two Chinese supertankers carrying 4 million barrels of crude exited the Strait of Hormuz after waiting two months, as US officials expressed optimism about negotiations with Tehran. (Al Jazeera)

item.title: ⚔️ Russia displays nuclear warhead transfers in major military exercise
item.description: Russia released footage of troops delivering nuclear warheads to mobile Iskander-M launch systems and moving them undetected to launch sites as part of a major military exercise. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Four US LNG vessels sailing to China following Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Four liquefied natural gas vessels are en route to China from the US, marking the first direct LNG cargoes during Trump's second term amid ongoing trade negotiations. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇱🇧 Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon kill 19, including children and women
item.description: Israeli airstrikes on southern Lebanon killed at least 19 people, including four women and three children, amid ongoing Israel-Hezbollah conflict despite U.S.-brokered ceasefire extensions. (The Hindu)

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ US Senate Advances Resolution to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
item.description: The US Senate moved forward with a procedural vote to curb presidential authority on Iran military actions, though the resolution faces significant hurdles before becoming law. (Reuters)

item.title: ☢️🇷🇺 Russia Conducts Major Nuclear Drills Amid Ongoing Tensions
item.description: Russia began three days of nuclear drills including ballistic and cruise missile launches, the defense ministry announced, as Moscow continues its confrontation with the West over Ukraine. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 NATO Commander: US Troop Cuts from Europe to Take Years
item.description: NATO's top commander said US troop withdrawals from Europe will stretch over years to give allies time to develop replacement capabilities, maintaining alliance defense posture. (Reuters)

item.title: ⚠️🇱🇻 Russia Warns Latvia Against Ukraine Drone Launches
item.description: Russia's UN ambassador claimed Ukraine plans to launch military drones from Latvia and other Baltic states, warning that NATO membership would not protect them from retaliation. (Reuters)

item.title: 💰🌐 G7 Finance Ministers Address Trade Imbalances, China Concerns
item.description: G7 finance ministers agreed on the need to tackle trade imbalances in a fragmented global economy, with US Treasury Secretary Bessent calling for protections against cheap Chinese imports. (Reuters)

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Xi Hosts Putin in Beijing, Hails Ties as "Calm Amid Chaos"
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed Vladimir Putin for a state visit just days after hosting Trump, calling Russia-China relations a force of stability. Xi urged an "early end" to the Iran war and criticized "unilateral hegemonic currents" in a veiled jab at the US. (CNN)

item.title: 🇺🇦 UN: Over 15,800 Killed in Russia-Ukraine War Since 2022
item.description: The UN reports at least 15,850 people including 791 children have been killed since Russia's full-scale invasion. The actual figures are likely significantly higher as peace talks remain stalled. (Al Jazeera)
